milestones: The output contains all five questions (Q1–Q5) with clearly labeled sections matching the required format
Question 1 states a single, specific research question directly related to personalized federated learning under statistical heterogeneity (Non-IID/unbalanced data)
Question 2 explains broader implications for the research community and discusses how solving the problem advances knowledge or enables practical applications
Question 3 identifies concrete technical or theoretical challenges and explains why naive or straightforward approaches would fail
Question 4 references specific gaps or limitations in prior work (e.g., FedAvg, FedProx, FedMA, or other cited methods) and clearly distinguishes the proposed approach from existing solutions
Question 5 describes a concrete methodology including at least a proposed method/algorithm, dataset(s) to be used, and evaluation metrics
